commit.message:
fix(yt-monitor): security fix, remove personal data, clean up dead files (#2)

- Fix code injection in mlx-whisper transcription by passing audio_path
  via sys.argv instead of embedding in f-string
- Move channels.json to ~/.wqq-skills/yt-monitor/ (runtime dir) to keep
  personal data out of the repo, with auto-init from example template
- Delete dead data/processed.json (script uses runtime dir copy)
- Remove unused notebook_id ghost field from add_channel()
- Update SKILL.md file structure and note Python usage rationale

fix: change OTP input length from 4 to 6 digits to match backend

Backend generates 6-digit OTPs but Pinput was set to 4 digits,
causing verify-otp to always fail with Invalid OTP.

commit.message:
回測引擎新增 20+ 專業量化指標（Sortino, Calmar, VaR, Hurst 等）

在 runBacktest() 加入完整機構級量化指標計算：
- 風險調整報酬：Sortino, Calmar, Omega
- 風險指標：VaR 95%/99%, CVaR, 年化波動率
- 統計特徵：Skewness, Kurtosis, Hurst Exponent, Tail Ratio
- 交易品質：Payoff Ratio, EV, Recovery Factor
- 持倉分析：最大連勝/連敗, 平均持有天數
- 痛苦指標：Ulcer Index, Pain Ratio

回測結果 UI 升級為分類儀表板（6 類 20+ 指標）

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update Finnish feature labels to match evaka UI terminology

Replace "kansalainen" with "kuntalainen" (4 labels) and align other
labels with evaka's actual i18n translations: use "varhaiskasvatusaika",
"varhaiskasvatuksen", "varhaiskasvatuspaikkatakuu", and "asiakirjapohjien".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove footer text from login page

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix natural-mode crop to output at native bounding-box dimensions

Previously, natural mode scaled the cropped region back up to 1024px wide,
making the output the same size as the source — no visible crop. Now the
output canvas is exactly srcW × srcH (the tight bounding box of non-transparent
pixels), producing a genuinely smaller image when the object doesn't fill the
full source canvas.

Square mode is unchanged: content is letterboxed inside canvasWidth × canvasWidth.

commit.message:
ci: set GOTOOLCHAIN=auto for golangci-lint action

Golangci-lint binary is compiled with go1.24 but go.mod targets
go1.26.1. Setting GOTOOLCHAIN=auto lets the tool auto-download
the required toolchain, matching the fix already applied to the
Dockerfile.

Add Batch 4 exam: 150 Java ITS/MTA-style questions

inheritance, interfaces, exceptions, collections, and more.
All four question types used: multiple-choice, true-false, yes-no, dropdown.

Agent reliability: timeout, /continue, multi-agent MCP fix

- Default agent timeout 10min → 30min, add per-agent --timeout flag
  (/agent --timeout 1h <prompt>)
- Add /continue command to resume finished/timed-out agents by label
  or ID, even after bridge restart (reads logs from disk)
- Fix MCP crash when multiple agents connect simultaneously: create
  one McpServer instance per SSE connection instead of sharing one
- Fix double notification on timeout (timed out + error)
- Track timeout/done/error messages in messageToAgent so reply-to
  works for follow-ups
- Improve agent system prompt: mention SSH access, cwd, timeout,
  and efficiency guidance

feat: Add automatic migration generation from schema diff (#7)

* feat: Add automatic migration generation from schema diff

Implement TypeORM-style auto-generation that compares current database
state with Drizzle entity definitions and generates migrations automatically.

Core Features:
- Schema introspection for PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite
- Schema loading from Drizzle entity files (supports multi-directory)
- Schema diffing engine to detect tables, columns, indexes, and FK changes
- SQL generation with dialect-specific DDL statements
- Auto-generated up() and down() migration methods
- Fallback to blank template when no schema changes detected

New Components:
- SchemaIntrospector: Read current database schema metadata (412 lines)
- SchemaLoader: Parse Drizzle table definitions from TS files (228 lines)
- SchemaDiffer: Compare schemas and detect changes (227 lines)
- SqlGenerator: Generate dialect-specific DDL (360 lines)

Configuration:
- Extended MigrationConfig with schemaFiles and dbCredentials
- MigrationGenerator now async to support database introspection
- Backward compatible with existing blank migration workflow

Testing:
- Added 46 unit tests (schema-differ, sql-generator)
- Added 53 integration tests (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite)
- All 99 tests passing with real database connections

Dependencies:
- Added drizzle-kit@^0.30.0 for schema parsing
- Added better-sqlite3@^11.0.0 for SQLite testing

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Fix case block lexical declaration error in generator.ts

- Auto-fix prettier/prettier formatting issues

Reduced lint issues from 65 to 43 (0 errors, 43 warnings).
Remaining warnings are acceptable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
warnings for dynamic database result handling.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: lint-check and lint-fix default to git-changed files, not whole dir

When called without an explicit path, both commands now use scope:git
to restrict linting to staged, unstaged, and new untracked Python files.
Pass an explicit path to override and check a specific directory.

commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Claude/fix athlete cards overflow bik k8 (#108)

* Fix iOS DOM corruption: CSS guardrail + MutationObserver replaces timing-dependent checks

Previous fixes used useEffect/setTimeout to detect orphaned <main> elements,
but these have a fundamental timing race — the check fires before or after
the corruption is detectable. This new approach uses two layers:

1. CSS `main:has(~ main) { display: none }` hides stale pages instantly
   before paint, with zero JS involvement
2. MutationObserver in ScrollRestorer proactively removes orphaned <main>
   nodes the moment they appear in the DOM

Removes checkDomIntegrity from ServiceWorkerRegistrar since the
MutationObserver now handles it continuously.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Myv3jTCMiyNePbtsZZZykT

* Revert aggressive DOM guardrails that crashed React on every page load

The MutationObserver in ScrollRestorer removed <main> elements from the DOM,
then React tried to removeChild on those same nodes and threw NotFoundError.
The CSS rule main:has(~ main) could also hide content during hydration.

Fix: remove both guardrails and restore a safe checkDomIntegrity() that only
runs on visibilitychange (page returning from background) and does a full
page reload instead of DOM surgery — safe because it's a clean restart.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Myv3jTCMiyNePbtsZZZykT

* Fix React crash: stop removing React-owned DOM nodes outside of React

Three components were calling .remove() on DOM nodes that React rendered,
causing NotFoundError when React tried to reconcile those same nodes:

1. SplashHider: splash.remove() → changed to display:none (node stays in DOM)
2. ScrollRestorer MutationObserver: removed <main> elements → removed entirely
3. CSS main:has(~ main): could hide content during hydration → removed

The safe checkDomIntegrity() in ServiceWorkerRegistrar (full page reload
on visibility change if multiple <main> detected) remains as the iOS
corruption guard — it doesn't touch React's DOM, just reloads cleanly.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Myv3jTCMiyNePbtsZZZykT

---------
